tagline: Everything here is soft. That is the rule.
elevator_pitch: Soft is a curated retail brand where every single product meets one criterion: it has to be soft. Blankets, pillows, loungewear, rugs, baby gifts, towels. If it is not impossibly soft, it does not make the cut. Think Bath and Body Works, but for texture lovers.
problem: Shopping for soft things is surprisingly hard. You order a blanket online and hope for the best. You squeeze a pillow through plastic packaging at the store and guess. Softness is one of the most desired qualities in home goods, bedding, and clothing, yet no retailer curates specifically for it. Bed Bath and Beyond is gone, and the void remains.
solution: Soft is a retail experience (both in-store and online) where every product has been hand-selected for maximum softness. Customers walk in, touch everything, and leave with exactly what they want. Online, every listing carries a verified softness rating. No guessing, no returns, no disappointment.
target_market: Home goods shoppers, gift buyers, new parents, newlyweds, and anyone who has ever said 'I just want something really soft.' Primary demographic: women aged 25-45 with disposable income who care about comfort and quality in their living spaces.
revenue_model: Retail margins on curated home goods, textiles, and loungewear. Phase one is a curation model with strong markup on partner brands. Phase two introduces private-label 'Soft' branded products manufactured directly, increasing margins to 60-70%. Gift bundles and subscription boxes provide recurring revenue.
key_features: Every product passes a strict softness curation standard before it hits the shelf
Tactile in-store experience designed for touching, feeling, and cozy discovery
Gift-ready collections for baby showers, weddings, housewarmings, and holidays
Private-label Soft brand products with premium materials at accessible prices
competitive_advantage: No retailer owns the 'soft' positioning. By building a brand entirely around one sensory quality, Soft becomes the default destination for anyone shopping by feel. The curation model starts lean and scales into private label, and the brand itself becomes the moat.
market_size_estimate: The U.S. home textiles market exceeds $30 billion annually. Pillows, blankets, towels, and bedding alone represent a $15 billion segment. A niche curation brand capturing even 0.1% of this market represents $15-30 million in annual revenue, with room to grow through private label and category expansion.
go_to_market: Launch as a direct-to-consumer e-commerce brand with a curated selection of 50-100 products. Build an Instagram-first marketing strategy showcasing the tactile, cozy brand aesthetic. Open a single flagship retail location in a high-traffic shopping district to validate the in-store experience. Partner with influencers in the home decor and lifestyle space.
risks: Softness is subjective; maintaining a consistent quality bar as the catalog grows is a challenge
Competing with established home goods retailers (Target, Crate & Barrel) on price and selection
Brick-and-mortar retail carries significant overhead and the in-store experience is core to the brand
year_one_milestones: Launch e-commerce store with 75+ curated products and achieve $500K in first-year revenue
Open one flagship retail location and validate the in-store soft experience concept
Introduce the first private-label Soft branded product line (blankets and pillows)
